summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of a blazing second set – the Wheel, funky Shakedown Street and stellar interplay make this show a fan favorite.
review: The recording is a solid soundboard source with minimal hiss but suffers from a few tape cuts (Jack Straw intro, Shakedown Street start, Never Trust a Woman and a reel‑cut Wharf Rat). Musically the second set shines – Garcia’s fire on The Wheel, Mydland’s funky keyboards on Shakedown Street, tight jams on Good Times, Spanish Jam and Saint of Circumstance keep listeners hooked.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (5‑star) rave about the performance while noting only minor recording glitches; strong consensus gives a high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Minimal analog hiss, generally clear mix. Notable issues: cut beginnings of Jack Straw, Shakedown Street and Never Trust a Woman; reel‑cut in Wharf Rat and missing ending of the show.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The second set is an uninterrupted, jam‑heavy journey that builds momentum from the pre‑drums Wheel through Shakedown Street and into extended improvisations, delivering a cohesive and uplifting groove.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is repeatedly described as "throwing serious heat", "almost speaking" on The Wheel and delivering funky wah‑wah tones; one reviewer notes his vocals (and Bob's) are muffled.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s whistle intro on Truckin' is highlighted; vocal clarity criticized by a single reviewer.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s keyboards provide "funky off‑speed" backing, delicate interplay on The Wheel and drive the Shakedown Street groove.
